steps.observation: P2 latency alert on search-service. p99 response time exceeded 3s.
Alert acknowledged. Investigating search-service degradation.
Logs show repeated elasticsearch query timeouts and GC pause warnings.
Elasticsearch heap usage at 96%. Old-gen GC cycles firing every 8 seconds.

action.postmortem_text: Elasticsearch heap exhaustion triggered frequent GC pauses, causing search-service query timeouts. Remediated by increasing heap allocation and optimizing index field mappings to reduce memory pressure.

steps.observation: P1 alert: payment-service error rate spiked to 42% over last 5 minutes.
Alert acknowledged. Checking payment-service logs.
Logs show fraud-detector returning 503s. Payment transactions failing at fraud check step.
fraud-detector logs show model-serving inference timeouts after 5s threshold.
model-serving CPU at 98%. Inference queue depth at 847 pending requests.

action.postmortem_text: CPU throttling on model-serving caused inference timeouts in fraud-detector, cascading to payment-service failures. Root cause was a batch scoring job consuming all available CPU. Remediated by isolating batch workloads to separate instance group.

steps.observation: P2 alert: auth-service login success rate dropped to 61%.
Alert acknowledged. Querying auth-service logs.
Logs show redis-cache connection pool exhausted. Sessions failing to read.
Redis active connections at 9,987 of 10,000 limit. Connection wait queue building.
Cache miss rate elevated at 34%. Many sessions not found, forcing DB fallback and holding connections longer.

action.postmortem_text: Redis connection saturation caused auth-service session lookups to fail. An expired key flush event caused a miss storm, increasing DB fallback latency and holding connections open, exhausting the pool. Fixed by increasing connection limit and implementing connection pooling on client side.

steps.observation: P3 alert: order-service p95 latency increased from 120ms to 890ms.
Logs show slow queries to postgres-primary. Transaction commit times elevated.
postgres-primary query latency p99 at 2.1s. Table lock contention detected on orders table.
Lock wait count at 312 concurrent waiters. Long-running analytics query holding exclusive lock.

action.postmortem_text: An unoptimized analytics query acquired table-level locks on the orders table, causing lock contention and elevated latency for order-service writes. Query was terminated and analytics workloads redirected to read replica.

steps.observation: P1 alert: api-gateway returning 502 errors at 18% of requests. Multiple downstream services affected.
Alert acknowledged. Querying api-gateway logs.
api-gateway logs show upstream connect failures to product-service and order-service. auth-service healthy.
product-service logs show postgres-primary connection refused errors. Service restarting repeatedly.
postgres-primary at max_connections limit of 500. Connection leak detected from product-service pool.

action.postmortem_text: A connection leak in product-service exhausted postgres-primary max_connections. product-service and order-service lost DB access, causing api-gateway 502s. Mitigated by restarting product-service to reclaim connections and setting idle connection timeout on pgBouncer.

steps.observation: P2 alert: notification-service delivery failure rate at 28% for the past 10 minutes.
Querying notification-service logs.
Logs show smtp-relay returning connection timeout after 30s. Email dispatch queue backing up.
smtp-relay showing 100% connection pool usage. Outbound TLS handshake failures to upstream MX servers.

action.postmortem_text: smtp-relay TLS certificate rotation caused handshake failures with upstream mail servers, exhausting connection pool. notification-service email deliveries failed as a result. Remediated by rolling back TLS certificate and scheduling proper rotation with pre-validation.

steps.observation: P2 alert: fraud-detector accuracy degraded, false positive rate spiked to 31%.
Querying fraud-detector logs.
Logs show model-serving returning stale model version after recent deployment. Feature pipeline mismatch detected.
model-serving running v2.1.3 but fraud-detector expects v2.2.0 feature schema. Input vector dimension mismatch causing fallback scoring.

action.postmortem_text: Canary deployment of fraud model v2.2.0 failed silently, leaving model-serving on v2.1.3. Feature schema mismatch caused fraud-detector to use degraded fallback scoring, elevating false positives. Resolved by completing model promotion and adding schema version validation to deployment pipeline.

steps.observation: P3 alert: product-service cache hit rate dropped from 89% to 41%.
Logs show redis-cache returning NOAUTH errors. Product service falling back to postgres for all reads.
Redis auth failure rate at 100% starting 22 minutes ago. Coincides with secret rotation event.
Cache miss rate at 97%. product-service is using stale Redis password from vault after rotation.

action.postmortem_text: Redis password rotation was not propagated to product-service configuration. Service continued using old credentials, causing all cache operations to fail with NOAUTH and saturating postgres-primary with reads. Resolved by redeploying product-service with updated secret reference and implementing dynamic secret reload.

steps.observation: P1 alert: payment-service transaction throughput dropped 78%. Revenue impact detected.
Alert acknowledged. Querying payment-service logs.
Logs show payment-db write errors. Deadlock detected on payment_transactions table.
payment-db deadlock rate at 47 per minute. Two conflicting transaction patterns acquiring locks in reverse order.
Query latency p99 at 8.3s. Deadlock victims rolling back and retrying, amplifying contention.

action.postmortem_text: A newly deployed payment reconciliation job acquired row locks in reverse order compared to the primary payment flow, causing deadlocks on payment_transactions table. Throughput collapsed due to rollback storms. Fixed by aligning lock acquisition order and adding deadlock retry backoff.

steps.observation: P2 alert: api-gateway JWT validation latency p99 jumped to 2.4s from 45ms baseline.
Querying api-gateway logs for JWT validation errors.
api-gateway logs show auth-service /validate endpoint timing out. Upstream timeout set to 2s.
auth-service logs show user-db query timeouts. Token validation requires user lookup on every request.
user-db query latency at 1.9s p99. Missing index on user_tokens table after recent schema migration.

action.postmortem_text: Schema migration dropped an index on user_tokens(token_hash) without recreating it. Full table scans on token validation caused user-db latency, propagating through auth-service to api-gateway JWT validation. Resolved by recreating index concurrently.

steps.observation: P2 alert: order-service checkout success rate at 73%, down from 99.1% baseline.
Querying order-service logs.
Logs show product-service returning 500s for inventory check calls. Order creation failing at stock validation step.
product-service logs show NullPointerException in inventory cache deserialization. Redis value format mismatch.
Redis eviction rate normal. Issue is deserialization of cached objects from prior product-service version incompatible with new schema.

action.postmortem_text: Rolling deployment of product-service introduced a breaking change to the cached inventory object schema. New pods failed to deserialize objects written by old pods, causing 500s on inventory checks and order failures. Resolved by flushing affected cache keys and ensuring backward-compatible serialization in deployment process.

steps.observation: P3 alert: elasticsearch indexing lag exceeds 4 minutes for product catalog updates.
search-service logs show elasticsearch bulk indexing returning 429 Too Many Requests errors.
Elasticsearch indexing rate throttled. Shard write queue depth at 98% capacity.
Heap usage at 88%. Indexing pressure combined with active search queries causing write rejection.

action.postmortem_text: Elasticsearch shard write queues filled due to combined heap pressure from concurrent search queries and a bulk reindex job. Write rejections caused indexing lag in search-service. Resolved by throttling the reindex job during peak hours and adding dedicated indexing nodes.

steps.observation: P1 alert: api-gateway error rate at 31%. Mixed 500 and 503 errors across all routes.
Alert acknowledged. Querying api-gateway logs.
Logs show circuit breaker open for order-service. auth-service and product-service healthy.
order-service OOMKilled. Pods restarting continuously. Heap dumps triggered.
order-service memory usage hit container limit of 2GB before OOM. Memory growth correlates with a new batch export endpoint deployed 12 minutes ago.

action.postmortem_text: A newly deployed batch export endpoint in order-service loaded entire order result sets into memory without pagination, causing OOM crashes. Repeated restarts triggered api-gateway circuit breaker. Resolved by rolling back the deployment and implementing cursor-based pagination for exports.

steps.observation: P2 alert: fraud-detector latency p99 increased to 4.1s. Payment approvals slowing.
Querying fraud-detector logs.
Logs show model-serving inference calls taking 3.8s. Normal baseline is 120ms.
model-serving memory usage at 94%. Memory pressure causing model weight swapping to disk.
Disk I/O at 98% utilization on model-serving. Memory exhaustion causing model paging, inflating inference latency.

action.postmortem_text: model-serving memory exhaustion caused model weights to be paged to disk, inflating inference time from 120ms to 4s. Root cause was loading two large model versions simultaneously during an A/B test. Resolved by unloading the shadow model and implementing model memory limits per deployment slot.

steps.observation: P3 alert: user-db replication lag at 47 seconds on read replica.
auth-service logs show stale session reads. Users seeing logged-out state after recent login.
Replication lag growing. Write-heavy batch import job running on primary causing WAL backlog.
Primary write throughput at 18,000 rows/second from import job. Replica apply thread single-threaded, falling behind.

action.postmortem_text: A large user data import job on user-db primary generated WAL volume exceeding replica apply capacity, causing 47-second replication lag. auth-service read replicas served stale session data. Resolved by pausing the import job, enabling parallel apply on replica, and routing auth reads to primary until lag cleared.

steps.observation: P2 alert: notification-service order confirmation delivery rate dropped to 54%.
Querying notification-service logs.
Logs show notification-service unable to fetch order details. order-service /orders/{id} returning 404 intermittently.
order-service logs show postgres-primary read timeouts causing 404 responses for recently created orders.
postgres-primary query latency at 3.2s p99 due to autovacuum running on orders table during peak traffic.

action.postmortem_text: Autovacuum triggered on the high-write orders table during peak hours, causing read query latency spikes. order-service timed out reading new orders, returning 404s to notification-service and causing delivery failures. Resolved by scheduling autovacuum during off-peak windows and tuning cost delay parameters.

steps.observation: P1 alert: payment-service fraud check bypass rate anomaly detected. 100% of transactions skipping fraud check.
Querying fraud-detector logs immediately.
fraud-detector returning 503. Service is down. payment-service configured to allow transactions when fraud check unavailable.
fraud-detector availability at 0%. Crash loop detected. payment-db connection string misconfigured after secret rotation.
payment-db shows zero connections from fraud-detector. Secret rotation left fraud-detector with invalid DSN.

action.postmortem_text: Secret rotation for payment-db credentials was not applied to fraud-detector configuration, causing crash loop on startup. payment-service failopen policy allowed all transactions through without fraud checks. Remediated by correcting secret binding, redeploying fraud-detector, and changing failopen policy to fail-closed with circuit breaker.

steps.observation: P3 alert: search-service query result quality degraded. Relevance scores abnormally low.
search-service logs show elasticsearch returning results from stale index alias. Recent product updates not reflected.
Active search alias pointing to index from 3 hours ago. Index swap cron job failed silently.
Elasticsearch logs show alias swap failed due to write lock timeout. Previous bulk indexing job held write lock longer than expected.

action.postmortem_text: The scheduled index alias swap cron job timed out waiting for a write lock held by an overrunning bulk indexing operation. search-service continued querying the stale alias, serving outdated product data. Fixed by manually swapping alias, adding lock timeout alerting, and making indexing jobs interruptible.

steps.observation: P2 alert: api-gateway rate limiting rejecting 23% of auth requests with 429 errors.
Querying api-gateway logs for rate limit source.
Rate limit buckets exhausted from a single API key. Appears to be a retry storm from a mobile client after auth-service returned 500s.
auth-service returned 500s due to user-db connection pool exhaustion 20 minutes ago. Mobile clients retried aggressively without backoff.
user-db connections now recovered. Retry storm secondary to original pool exhaustion which has self-resolved.

action.postmortem_text: Transient user-db connection pool exhaustion caused auth-service 500s. Mobile client retry logic without exponential backoff triggered a retry storm, exhausting api-gateway rate limit buckets. Primary issue self-resolved; mitigated retry storm by temporarily increasing rate limit for affected API key class and enforcing client-side backoff via SDK update.

steps.observation: P2 alert: product-service image CDN requests failing. Product images not loading for 34% of requests.
product-service logs show postgres-primary returning incorrect image URL format. Data corruption in image_assets table.
Replication integrity check passed. Issue isolated to data written by a recent migration script that used incorrect URL template.
Migration ran 90 minutes ago and corrupted 12,847 image_url fields with malformed paths. No replication issue.

action.postmortem_text: A data migration script used an incorrect URL template, corrupting 12,847 image_url rows in the image_assets table. product-service served malformed image URLs causing CDN 404s. Resolved by running a corrective SQL update and adding migration dry-run validation to deployment pipeline.

steps.observation: P1 alert: order-service and payment-service both down. Full checkout funnel unavailable.
Alert acknowledged. Querying order-service logs first.
order-service failing to connect to postgres-primary. Connection refused on port 5432.
payment-service also failing to connect to payment-db. Both databases unreachable.
postgres-primary and payment-db both showing 0% availability. Network ACL change deployed 8 minutes ago blocked DB subnet access from application tier.

action.postmortem_text: A network ACL change blocked TCP port 5432 between the application tier and database subnet, taking down postgres-primary and payment-db connectivity simultaneously. order-service and payment-service became unavailable. Resolved by reverting ACL change and implementing change validation for network rules.

steps.observation: P3 alert: redis-cache memory usage at 87%, approaching eviction threshold.
Redis memory at 87% and growing. Key count increased 3x in last 2 hours.
auth-service logs show session keys being written without TTL. Infinite-lived session keys accumulating.
Eviction rate zero. No TTLs set on session keys deployed in auth-service v3.4.1 released 2 hours ago.

action.postmortem_text: auth-service v3.4.1 introduced a regression that omitted TTL on session key writes, causing indefinite key accumulation in Redis. Memory grew toward eviction threshold, risking cache eviction of critical keys. Resolved by rolling back auth-service, purging keyspace of affected sessions, and adding TTL assertion in session write unit tests.

steps.observation: P2 alert: search-service returning empty results for 11% of product queries.
Querying search-service logs.
Logs show elasticsearch shard unavailable errors. Several shards in UNASSIGNED state.
5 primary shards unassigned. One elasticsearch data node went down 25 minutes ago. Replication factor insufficient for node failure.
Cluster at 2 of 3 data nodes. Unassigned shards are replicas of data on lost node. Data available but cluster degraded.

action.postmortem_text: One elasticsearch data node failed, causing 5 primary shards to become unassigned due to replica configuration with replication factor of 1. search-service returned empty results for queries routed to unavailable shards. Resolved by adding a replacement data node, triggering shard reallocation, and increasing replication factor to 2.

steps.observation: P2 alert: payment-service p95 latency at 6.2s. Payment processing slowing significantly.
Querying payment-service logs.
Logs show payment-db queries slow. Fraud-detector calls completing normally.
payment-db query latency at 5.8s p99. Index bloat detected on payment_transactions.
payment_transactions primary index size grown to 47GB with 68% bloat factor. VACUUM not run in 14 days.

action.postmortem_text: Index bloat on payment_transactions table caused sequential scan fallback for several query patterns, inflating payment-db latency. VACUUM had not run due to a lock contention issue preventing autovacuum. Resolved by running REINDEX CONCURRENTLY and restoring autovacuum operation with adjusted lock timeout.

steps.observation: P3 alert: auth-service token generation latency increased to 340ms from 12ms baseline.
auth-service logs show RSA key signing operations taking 320ms each. CPU-bound.
auth-service CPU at 96%. Token generation using RSA-4096 instead of expected RSA-2048 after key rotation.
Signing key rotated to RSA-4096 by security team. Signing operations 8x more CPU intensive, saturating service CPUs.

action.postmortem_text: Security team rotated JWT signing keys to RSA-4096 without coordinating capacity impact assessment. auth-service CPU saturated due to 8x increase in signing cost. Resolved by switching to ECDSA P-256 providing equivalent security with lower computational overhead and horizontal scaling of auth-service pods.

steps.observation: P2 alert: model-serving inference failure rate at 14% for fraud scoring requests.
Querying model-serving logs.
Logs show CUDA out of memory errors on 2 of 4 GPU instances. Batch size limit not enforced.
GPU memory at 100% on 2 instances. Large batch requests from fraud-detector overwhelming GPU memory budget.
fraud-detector sending batches of 512 samples per request. Model GPU budget assumes max batch size of 64.

action.postmortem_text: fraud-detector increased batch size to 512 for throughput optimization without coordinating with model-serving capacity. GPU memory exhaustion on 2 instances caused inference failures and CUDA OOM errors. Resolved by enforcing server-side batch size limits in model-serving and establishing batch size SLOs between services.

steps.observation: P2 alert: smtp-relay bounce rate at 41%. Notification delivery quality degraded.
Querying notification-service logs for bounce patterns.
Logs show smtp-relay being blacklisted by major mail providers. Bounce messages include IP reputation failure.
smtp-relay egress IP flagged on Spamhaus. Caused by a notification-service bulk send to unverified email list.
2 hours ago, 890,000 emails sent to unverified marketing list. Spam trap hits caused IP blacklisting.

action.postmortem_text: A marketing bulk send to an unverified email list triggered spam trap hits and caused smtp-relay egress IPs to be blacklisted by major providers. notification-service transactional delivery collaterally impacted. Resolved by rotating to clean egress IPs, requesting delisting, and enforcing email list hygiene gates before bulk sends.

steps.observation: P1 alert: api-gateway health check failing. All routes returning 503.
Alert acknowledged. Querying api-gateway logs.
api-gateway logs show TLS certificate expired 4 minutes ago. All HTTPS connections failing.
TLS certificate expired at 14:32 UTC. Certificate auto-renewal cron job failed 3 days ago silently. No alerting on renewal failure.

action.postmortem_text: api-gateway TLS certificate expired due to silent failure of automated renewal cron job 3 days prior. All HTTPS traffic rejected upon expiry, causing complete service outage. Resolved by emergency certificate renewal via manual ACME challenge and adding monitoring for certificate renewal job success and 14-day expiry pre-alert.

steps.observation: P3 alert: product-service response time degraded for category listing endpoints specifically.
Logs show category listing queries executing full table scan on products table. Explain plan changed.
Query planner switched from index scan to seq scan after statistics became stale. ANALYZE not run post-bulk-insert.
products table statistics 6 days old. 2.1M rows inserted via data import yesterday, statistics badly out of date.

action.postmortem_text: Stale table statistics after a large data import caused the PostgreSQL query planner to choose sequential scans over index scans for category listing queries. product-service latency degraded for those endpoints. Resolved by running ANALYZE on products table and adding ANALYZE step to data import runbooks.

steps.observation: P2 alert: fraud-detector false negative rate elevated. Fraud transactions passing through undetected.
Querying fraud-detector logs.
Logs show fraud-detector using cached feature values from payment-db that are 2 hours stale due to read replica lag.
payment-db replica lag at 118 minutes. fraud-detector feature queries routing to replica to reduce primary load.
payment-db primary write throughput 3x normal due to end-of-month settlement batch. Replica severely behind.

action.postmortem_text: End-of-month settlement batch caused elevated write throughput on payment-db primary, creating 118-minute replication lag on replica. fraud-detector read stale features from replica, degrading detection accuracy. Resolved by routing fraud feature reads to primary during settlement batches and implementing lag-based replica read routing.

steps.observation: P2 alert: order-service creating duplicate orders. Idempotency violation detected.
Querying order-service logs for duplicate creation events.
Logs show idempotency keys not being checked due to redis-cache returning errors. Falling back to non-idempotent path.
Redis availability at 67%. Cluster failover in progress after primary node failure. Some key lookups failing during election.
Redis sentinel election completed 3 minutes ago. New primary elected. Idempotency key lookups now succeeding.

action.postmortem_text: Redis primary node failure triggered sentinel failover. During the 4-minute election window, idempotency key lookups failed. order-service fallback path did not enforce idempotency, causing duplicate order creation for requests retried during failover. Resolved by deduplicating orders, hardening idempotency fallback to queue rather than proceed, and reducing Redis sentinel election timeout.

steps.observation: P2 alert: api-gateway upstream timeout rate to auth-service at 8% over last 5 minutes.
Querying auth-service logs.
auth-service logs show deadlock on user_sessions table in user-db. Multiple concurrent session update operations.
user-db deadlock rate at 22 per minute on user_sessions table. Concurrent last_seen_at updates conflicting.
user-db latency at 1.8s p99 due to deadlock rollbacks. Auth transactions taking multiple retries.

action.postmortem_text: Concurrent last_seen_at timestamp updates on user_sessions caused deadlocks when multiple requests updated the same row simultaneously. auth-service retries compounded latency, causing api-gateway timeouts. Resolved by making last_seen_at updates asynchronous via a background job and adding deadlock-aware retry logic with jitter.

steps.observation: P3 alert: elasticsearch cluster disk usage at 83%. Approaching 85% watermark that triggers read-only mode.
Elasticsearch disk at 83% and growing 2% per hour. Index retention policy not deleting old indices.
ILM policy for log indices failed to delete 47 indices older than 30 days. ILM executor throwing permission errors.
ILM role permissions revoked during security audit 2 days ago. Delete operation denied since then.

action.postmortem_text: A security audit revoked elasticsearch ILM role delete permissions, preventing index lifecycle management from deleting aged indices. Disk usage grew toward read-only threshold. Resolved by restoring ILM role permissions, manually deleting overdue indices, and adding ILM execution success monitoring.

steps.observation: P2 alert: payment-service 3DS authentication success rate dropped to 71%. Card declines increasing.
Querying payment-service logs.
Logs show 3DS redirect URLs malformed. Callback domain changed after infrastructure migration but not updated in payment-db configuration.
payment-db holds old callback domain config. Infrastructure migration changed domain 6 hours ago. 3DS providers unable to reach callback endpoint.

action.postmortem_text: Infrastructure domain migration changed the 3DS callback URL but payment-db configuration was not updated, causing card issuers to receive malformed redirect URLs. 3DS authentication failures caused elevated card declines. Resolved by updating payment-db 3DS configuration and adding domain change validation to infrastructure migration runbook.

steps.observation: P2 alert: product-service search integration returning zero results for all queries using product attributes.
product-service logs show search-service returning 400 errors for attribute-based queries. Field name mismatch in request payload.
search-service logs show elasticsearch returning mapping errors. Field 'product_attributes' renamed to 'attributes' in new index mapping.
Elasticsearch index mapping updated to v7 with field rename. product-service still sending v6 field names.

action.postmortem_text: Elasticsearch index mapping was updated with a breaking field rename without coordinating with product-service. product-service sent deprecated field names causing 400 errors from search-service and zero attribute-based search results. Resolved by rolling back mapping change, implementing a compatibility layer, and establishing mapping change review process.

steps.observation: P1 alert: notification-service processing queue depth at 847,000. Critical backlog building.
Querying notification-service logs.
Logs show notification-service workers blocked waiting on order-service API for order detail enrichment.
order-service response time at 12s due to postgres-primary experiencing high load. Notification workers timing out and retrying.
postgres-primary CPU at 97%. Long-running report query holding shared locks on orders table.

action.postmortem_text: An ad-hoc analytics report query caused postgres-primary CPU saturation and lock contention. order-service latency spiked to 12s, blocking notification-service worker threads. Queue depth grew to 847,000. Resolved by terminating the blocking query, horizontally scaling notification workers, and routing analytics queries to read replica.

steps.observation: P2 alert: auth-service returning 401 for valid sessions after deployment at 09:14 UTC.
Querying auth-service logs.
Logs show JWT signature validation failing. HMAC secret mismatch between token issuer and validator.
Validation failure rate at 38%. Affects only tokens issued before 09:14. New deployment loaded a different HMAC secret from vault.
Confirmed: old pods used secret version v3, new pods loaded v4 after deployment. Sessions issued by old pods now fail validation on new pods.

action.postmortem_text: auth-service deployment loaded an updated HMAC secret version from vault, causing JWT tokens issued by previous pods to fail validation on new pods. Users were logged out mid-session. Resolved by rolling back secret version, implementing dual-key validation window during rotations, and adding pre-deployment secret compatibility checks.

steps.observation: P3 alert: model-serving inference throughput dropped 40% with no error rate increase.
Throughput down from 1,200 to 720 requests/second. Latency stable. Batch sizes unchanged.
CPU throttled at 60% of previous capacity. Instance type downscaled in autoscaling policy change.
Logs confirm autoscaler switched instance class from c5.4xlarge to c5.2xlarge 90 minutes ago. Cost optimization change applied without load testing.

action.postmortem_text: An autoscaling policy change downgraded model-serving instances from c5.4xlarge to c5.2xlarge for cost savings without validating throughput impact. Available CPU halved, reducing inference throughput by 40%. Resolved by restoring original instance class and establishing load testing requirements before autoscaling policy changes.

steps.observation: P2 alert: api-gateway request success rate at 82%. Mixed service degradation pattern.
Querying api-gateway logs to identify failing routes.
Failures concentrated on /products/* and /orders/* routes. /auth/* routes healthy.
product-service logs show redis-cache returning WRONGTYPE errors. String operations called on hash keys.
Redis error rate elevated. A cache key naming collision introduced in product-service v2.9.0 overwrote hash keys with string values.

action.postmortem_text: product-service v2.9.0 introduced a cache key naming collision where new string-type keys overwrote existing hash-type keys. Subsequent hash operations on those keys returned WRONGTYPE errors, causing product-service failures propagating to api-gateway. Resolved by rolling back v2.9.0, flushing conflicting keys, and adding key type assertions to cache layer tests.

steps.observation: P2 alert: order-service inventory reservation success rate at 68%. Orders failing at stock check.
Querying order-service logs.
order-service calling product-service inventory API getting 503s. product-service pod count dropped.
product-service down to 2 of 12 pods. HPA scaling blocked. Resource quota exhausted in namespace.
Kubernetes namespace CPU quota at 100%. Competing workload deployed to same namespace consumed available quota, blocking product-service scale-out.

action.postmortem_text: A test workload deployed to the production namespace consumed available CPU resource quota, preventing product-service HPA from scaling out during traffic increase. Only 2 of 12 desired pods ran, causing 503s and order inventory check failures. Resolved by evicting test workload, expanding resource quota, and enforcing namespace isolation policy.

steps.observation: P1 alert: payment-service transaction failure rate at 67%. Widespread payment outage.
Alert acknowledged. Querying payment-service logs.
Logs show payment-db SSL certificate verification failure. All DB connections rejected.
payment-db server certificate expired 22 minutes ago. Certificate auto-renewal failed due to DNS validation issue.
Renewal agent logs confirm DNS-01 challenge failed. DNS record propagation delayed due to low TTL issue.

action.postmortem_text: payment-db TLS certificate expired after automated renewal via DNS-01 ACME challenge failed due to DNS propagation delays from a low TTL misconfiguration. All payment-service DB connections rejected, causing payment outage. Resolved by manual certificate emergency issuance, fixing DNS TTL, and adding 30-day pre-expiry certificate monitoring.

steps.observation: P3 alert: search-service query latency p50 increased from 45ms to 320ms for full-text queries.
search-service logs show elasticsearch queries timing out. High query complexity detected in slow query log.
Elasticsearch slow query log showing wildcard prefix queries taking 800ms each. New search feature using leading wildcards.
Heap usage at 74% but stable. Performance issue is query pattern, not resource exhaustion.

action.postmortem_text: A new search feature deployed leading-wildcard queries which cannot use elasticsearch inverted index and require full shard scan. Query latency increased 7x under normal load. Resolved by replacing leading-wildcard queries with edge n-gram tokenizer at index time for prefix search capability.

steps.observation: P2 alert: notification-service failing to send order status updates. 0 emails sent in last 15 minutes.
Querying notification-service logs.
Logs show notification-service consuming empty events from order-service topic. No order events published in last 18 minutes.
order-service logs show Kafka producer errors. Broker connection failures after network partition.
Kafka producer error rate at 100% for 18 minutes. order-service unable to reach Kafka broker after AZ network issue. Events silently dropped.

action.postmortem_text: A network partition in one availability zone severed order-service connectivity to the Kafka broker. Event publication silently failed without dead-letter queue, causing notification-service to starve of events and halt email delivery. Resolved by restoring network, replaying missed events from order-service database, and implementing Kafka producer DLQ and producer health alerting.

steps.observation: P2 alert: fraud-detector scoring latency p99 at 7.2s. Payment approvals severely impacted.
Querying fraud-detector logs.
Logs show payment-db feature queries slow. Transaction history lookups taking 6.8s.
payment-db query latency at 6.9s for transaction history aggregation. Index on user_id dropped during schema migration.
Index on payment_transactions(user_id, created_at) missing. Migration script omitted index recreation after table rebuild.

action.postmortem_text: A schema migration that rebuilt the payment_transactions table omitted recreating the compound index on (user_id, created_at). fraud-detector feature queries fell back to sequential scans, causing 6.8s latency and payment approval delays. Resolved by creating index concurrently and adding index verification step to migration scripts.

steps.observation: P2 alert: api-gateway upstream health checks failing for product-service. 3 of 8 instances unhealthy.
Querying product-service logs on unhealthy instances.
Logs on 3 instances show JVM stop-the-world GC pauses exceeding health check timeout of 5s. Instances marked unhealthy.
GC pause times at 8-12 seconds on affected instances. Heap at 91%. Memory leak in product cache implementation.
Heap growing linearly at 120MB/hour. Memory leak traced to product-service cache not releasing image metadata objects.

action.postmortem_text: A memory leak in product-service image metadata cache caused heap to grow to 91%, triggering stop-the-world GC pauses exceeding health check timeouts. 3 instances removed from load balancer rotation. Resolved by rolling restart of affected instances, patching cache eviction logic, and adding heap growth rate alerting.

steps.observation: P1 alert: user-db primary unreachable. auth-service and product-service both degraded.
Alert acknowledged. Querying auth-service logs.
auth-service logs show user-db connection refused. Failover not yet promoted.
user-db primary down. Automated failover triggered 4 minutes ago. Replica promotion taking longer than expected.
Replica has 2-minute replication lag to catch up before promotion completes. Estimated 90 seconds until new primary available.

action.postmortem_text: user-db primary experienced hardware failure, triggering automated replica promotion. A 2-minute replication lag delayed promotion by 3.5 minutes beyond RTO target. auth-service and product-service were unavailable during failover window. Post-incident actions: reduce replication lag via synchronous replication for user-db, reduce failover detection timeout, and pre-warm connection pools on standby.

steps.observation: P3 alert: redis-cache hit ratio for auth sessions degraded from 94% to 61%.
Cache hit ratio at 61%. Evictions spiked 45 minutes ago and not recovered.
Evictions at 2,300/second. maxmemory policy is allkeys-lru. Auth session keys being evicted by large ephemeral job keys.
Auth-service logs show session cache misses forcing user-db lookups. Ephemeral batch job added 18GB of large keys 45 minutes ago filling Redis memory.

action.postmortem_text: A batch processing job wrote 18GB of large temporary keys to Redis, causing allkeys-lru eviction to evict auth session keys. Cache hit ratio for auth sessions dropped 33 points, increasing user-db load. Resolved by moving batch job keys to a dedicated Redis instance and setting appropriate TTLs on temporary keys.

steps.observation: P2 alert: order-service failing to process refund requests. Refund success rate at 0% for last 12 minutes.
Querying order-service logs for refund processing errors.
Logs show payment-service /refund endpoint returning 422 Unprocessable Entity. Payload validation failing.
payment-service logs show refund request missing required currency_code field. Breaking API change deployed to payment-service without versioning.
payment-service v4.1.0 added required currency_code field. order-service on v4.0.x contract does not send this field.

action.postmortem_text: payment-service v4.1.0 introduced a breaking change by adding a required currency_code field to the refund API without backward compatibility or API versioning. order-service sent requests missing the field, causing 422 rejections and total refund failure. Resolved by making currency_code optional with default, implementing API versioning, and requiring contract tests in CI before payment-service deployments.

steps.observation: P2 alert: search-service autocomplete latency at 1.8s. Users experiencing slow search suggestions.
search-service logs show elasticsearch completion suggester taking 1.6s. Normal is 15ms.
Elasticsearch heap at 91%. Completion suggester FST data structures fully in heap. GC pressure affecting all query types.
GC pauses at 400ms. Completion suggester index grew 8x after product catalog expansion last week.

action.postmortem_text: Elasticsearch completion suggester FST structures grew proportionally with a product catalog expansion, consuming 91% of heap and causing GC pressure that elevated autocomplete latency from 15ms to 1.8s. Resolved by pruning low-frequency suggestion entries, increasing heap allocation on dedicated suggest nodes, and separating suggest index from primary product index.

steps.observation: P1 alert: complete checkout flow unavailable. api-gateway returning 503 on all order and payment routes for 6 minutes.
Alert acknowledged. Querying api-gateway logs to identify root scope.
api-gateway logs show service mesh sidecar rejecting requests with mTLS handshake failures on order-service and payment-service routes.
mTLS workload certificates expired on order-service and payment-service pods. Certificate rotation agent crashed and not restarted.
Both services have expired mTLS certs. Rotation agent pod in CrashLoopBackOff due to OOM since 2 hours before expiry.

action.postmortem_text: The service mesh mTLS certificate rotation agent entered CrashLoopBackOff due to a memory leak in the agent itself, preventing renewal of workload certificates. order-service and payment-service certs expired, causing all mTLS handshakes to fail and complete checkout outage. Resolved by restarting and patching the rotation agent, performing emergency certificate renewal, and adding rotation agent health as a critical alert.
